quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Nomenclature and Formula Writing
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Naming Ionic Compounds with Variable Charges, Polyatomic Ions, Molecular Compound Naming, Stock System, IUPAC Nomenclature

questions.question: What is the correct name for $\text{PbO}_2$?
questions.explanation: $\text{PbO}_2$ is named lead(IV) oxide. Oxygen has a -2 charge, so two oxygens give -4. Therefore, lead must have a +4 charge, indicated by (IV).

questions.question: What is the correct name for $(\text{NH}_4)_2\text{CO}_3$?
questions.explanation: $(\text{NH}_4)_2\text{CO}_3$ is named ammonium carbonate. It contains the ammonium ion ($\text{NH}_4^+$) and the carbonate ion ($\text{CO}_3^{2-}$).

questions.question: Which polyatomic ion has the greatest negative charge?
questions.explanation: $\text{PO}_4^{3-}$ (phosphate) has a 3- charge, which is the greatest negative charge among these options. $\text{NO}_3^-$ is 1-, $\text{SO}_4^{2-}$ is 2-, and $\text{OH}^-$ is 1-.

questions.question: What is the correct formula for iron(III) nitrate?
questions.explanation: Iron(III) nitrate is $\text{Fe(NO}_3)_3$. Iron(III) has a +3 charge ($\text{Fe}^{3+}$), and nitrate is $\text{NO}_3^-$ with a -1 charge, so three nitrates are needed.

questions.question: What is the name of $\text{ClO}_3^-$?
questions.explanation: $\text{ClO}_3^-$ is named chlorate. The pattern for chlorine oxyanions is: $\text{ClO}^-$ (hypochlorite), $\text{ClO}_2^-$ (chlorite), $\text{ClO}_3^-$ (chlorate), $\text{ClO}_4^-$ (perchlorate).

questions.question: What is the correct formula for ammonium chloride?
questions.explanation: Ammonium chloride is $\text{NH}_4\text{Cl}$. Ammonium ($\text{NH}_4^+$) has a +1 charge, and chloride ($\text{Cl}^-$) has a -1 charge, so they combine in a 1:1 ratio.

questions.question: What is the correct name for $\text{Ca(OH)}_2$?
questions.explanation: $\text{Ca(OH)}_2$ is named calcium hydroxide. It contains the hydroxide ion ($\text{OH}^-$). Calcium has a +2 charge, so two hydroxide ions are needed.

questions.question: What is the correct formula for aluminum sulfate?
questions.explanation: Aluminum sulfate is $\text{Al}_2(\text{SO}_4)_3$. Aluminum has a +3 charge ($\text{Al}^{3+}$), and sulfate is $\text{SO}_4^{2-}$ with a -2 charge. To balance charges, two $\text{Al}^{3+}$ (+6) combine with three $\text{SO}_4^{2-}$ (-6).

questions.question: Which of the following gives the correct chemical formula for the compound Magnesium Phosphide?
questions.explanation: Magnesium phosphide is $\text{Mg}_3\text{P}_2$. Magnesium (Group 2) forms $\text{Mg}^{2+}$, and phosphorus (Group 15) forms $\text{P}^{3-}$ (phosphide). To balance charges, three $\text{Mg}^{2+}$ (+6) combine with two $\text{P}^{3-}$ (-6).

questions.question: Which of the following gives the correct chemical formula for the compound Calcium Nitride?
questions.explanation: Calcium nitride is $\text{Ca}_3\text{N}_2$. Calcium (Group 2) forms $\text{Ca}^{2+}$, and nitrogen (Group 15) forms $\text{N}^{3-}$ (nitride). To balance charges, three $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ (+6) combine with two $\text{N}^{3-}$ (-6).

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the ionic compound: $\text{Fe}_3\text{N}_2$?
questions.explanation: $\text{Fe}_3\text{N}_2$ is named iron(II) nitride. Nitrogen ($\text{N}^{3-}$) has a -3 charge, so two nitrogens give -6. Therefore, three irons must total +6, meaning each iron has a +2 charge, indicated by (II).

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the ionic compound: $\text{CuS}$?
questions.explanation: $\text{CuS}$ is named copper(II) sulfide. Sulfur ($\text{S}^{2-}$) has a -2 charge, so copper must have a +2 charge. Copper(I) sulfide would be $\text{Cu}_2\text{S}$.

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the molecular compound: $\text{CS}_2$?
questions.explanation: $\text{CS}_2$ is named carbon disulfide. It is a covalent compound, so prefixes are used. The 'di-' prefix indicates two sulfur atoms. The 'mono-' prefix for carbon is omitted.

questions.question: What is the correct chemical name for the molecular compound: $\text{SF}_4$?
questions.explanation: $\text{SF}_4$ is named sulfur tetrafluoride. It is a covalent compound, so prefixes are used. The 'tetra-' prefix indicates four fluorine atoms. Roman numerals are not used for molecular compounds.

questions.question: Which of these is known as Tetraoxosulfate (VI) acid?
questions.explanation: Sulfuric acid ($\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$) is known systematically as tetraoxosulfate(VI) acid. The name indicates a central sulfur atom in the +6 oxidation state bonded to four oxygen atoms (tetraoxo).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered advanced nomenclature and formula writing! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review charge balancing for compounds with polyatomic ions and transition metals.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on common polyatomic ions and determining oxidation states.
scoreMessages.0: Please review naming rules for ionic compounds with variable charges, polyatomic ions, and molecular compounds.
